title: Trapping Rain Water
pattern: Two Pointers â€” Bounded by Max Height on Each Side
coreIdea: Calculate total trapped water across an elevation map by tracking the maximum height seen from the left and right at each position; water trapped at any bar equals min(leftMax, rightMax) minus the bar's own height.
recognitionCues: asks for total water trapped between bars
approach: Use two pointers from both ends, tracking leftMax and rightMax. Move the pointer on the side with the smaller max inward, since that side's water level is bounded by its own max; add (that max - current height) to the total at each step.
